---
title: "Assignmnet-5"
author: "Tushar Sharma - 25040208013"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

# Learning Data transformation with dplyr

#### Dplyr-

-   Pick observations by their values (filter()).

-   Reorder the rows (arrange()).

-   Pick variables by their names (select()).

-   Create new variables with functions of existing variables (mutate()).

-   Collapse many values down to a single summary (summarize())

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(nycflights13)
library(dplyr)
```

### Filter Rows with Filter()

```{r}
#Filter helps to select all flights on January 1st
filter(flights,month==1,day==1)
```

#### We can store this filter data in some variable

```{r}
jan1 <- filter(flights, month == 1, day == 1)
```

#### Along with storing in a variable we can same time print it also by *wrap the assignment in parentheses*

```{r}
(dec25 <- filter(flights, month == 12, day == 25))
```

### Logical Operators

#### In filter(), multiple conditions use AND by default, so all must be TRUE.

#### Use & for and, \| for or, and ! for not.

![](images/Screenshot%202026-02-11%20145606.png)

### Filtering Flights for November or December

To filter rows for multiple values, we must explicitly compare the column each time.

```{r}
# Correct way: explicitly compare month to both values
# month == 11 | month == 12 ensures each condition is checked separately
filter(flights, month == 11 | month == 12)

```

```{r}
# Better way: %in% checks if month belongs to the given vector
# This is cleaner and avoids logical confusion
nov_dec <- filter(flights, month %in% c(11, 12))

```

### Missing Values

-   `NA` represents a missing or unknown value in R.

-   Any operation involving `NA` usually returns `NA` because the value is unknown.

##### 1. Operation with NA

```{r}
# Comparisons with NA return NA
print(NA > 5)
print(10 == NA)
print(NA + 10)
print(NA / 2)

```

##### 2. Comparing NA with NA

```{r}
# NA is not equal to NA because both values are unknown
print(NA == NA)

```

##### 3. Using Variables with NA

```{r}
# Example with unknown values
x <- NA
y <- NA

print(x == y)   # We don't know if they are equal
```

##### 4. Checking Missing Values using is.na()

```{r}
# is.na() checks whether a value is missing
print(is.na(x))
```

## Filtering with NA using dplyr

-   `filter()` only keeps rows where the condition is TRUE.\
    It removes both FALSE and NA values.

#### 5. Filtering Without Including NA

```{r}
df <- tibble(x = c(1, NA, 3))

# This excludes NA because NA > 1 is unknown
filter(df, x > 1)
```

```{r}
# Explicitly include missing departure delays
filter(flights, is.na(dep_delay) | dep_delay > 60)

```

#### Missing values - sorted at the end:

```{r}
df <- tibble(x= c(5,2,NA))
arrange(df,x)
```

#### Select Columns with select()

```{r}
# Select columns by name
select(flights, year, month, day)
```

```{r}
# Select all columns between year and day (inclusive)
select(flights, year:day)
```

```{r}
# Select all columns except those from year to day (inclusive)
select(flights, -(year:day))
```

### Add New Variables with mutate()

```{r}
flights_sml <- select(flights,
year:day,
ends_with("delay"),
distance,
air_time
)
mutate(flights_sml,
gain = arr_delay - dep_delay,
speed = distance / air_time * 60
)
```

```{r}
#Note that you can refer to columns that you’ve just created:
mutate(flights_sml,
gain = arr_delay - dep_delay,
hours = air_time / 60,
gain_per_hour = gain / hours
)
```

```{r}
#If you only want to keep the new variables, use transmute():
transmute(flights,
gain = arr_delay - dep_delay,
hours = air_time / 60,
gain_per_hour = gain / hours
)
```

### Grouped Summaries with summarize()

```{r}
summarize(flights, delay = mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE))
```

#### summarize() are more usefull when we pair it with group_by()

```{r}
by_day <- group_by(flights, year, month, day)
summarize(by_day, delay = mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE))
```

## Combining Multiple Operations with Pipe

The pipe `%>%` is used to perform multiple operations in sequence.\
It makes the code easier to read from top to bottom.

##### `%>%` can be read as “then”

-   how it looks without pipe

```{r}
by_dest <- group_by(flights, dest)

delay <- summarize(by_dest,
                   count = n(),
                   dist = mean(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
                   delay = mean(arr_delay, na.rm = TRUE))

delay <- filter(delay, count > 20, dest != "HNL")

ggplot(delay, aes(x = dist, y = delay)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = count), alpha = 1/3) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE)
```

### With Pipe

The pipe passes the result of one step to the next.

```{r}
delays <- flights %>%
  group_by(dest) %>%
  summarize(
    count = n(),
    dist = mean(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
    delay = mean(arr_delay, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(count > 20, dest != "HNL")

ggplot(delays, aes(x = dist, y = delay)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = count), alpha = 1/3) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE)

```

### Counts

-   Counts help us understand how much data is in each group.

-   We can count total rows, non-missing values, distinct values, and logical conditions.

```{r}
# Remove cancelled flights
not_cancelled <- flights %>% 
  filter(!is.na(dep_delay), !is.na(arr_delay))

# 1. Count number of flights per destination
not_cancelled %>%
  group_by(dest) %>%
  summarize(n = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = delays, mapping = aes(x = delay)) +
geom_freqpoly(binwidth = 10)
```

```{r}
# 2. Count number of non-missing arrival delays
not_cancelled %>%
  summarize(non_missing = sum(!is.na(arr_delay)))

```

```{r}
# 3. Count distinct carriers per destination
not_cancelled %>%
  group_by(dest) %>%
  summarize(carriers = n_distinct(carrier)) %>%
  arrange(desc(carriers))

```

```{r}
# 4. Simple count using count()
not_cancelled %>%
  count(dest)

```

```{r}
# 5. Weighted count (total distance flown per plane)
not_cancelled %>%
  count(tailnum, wt = distance)

```

```{r}
# 6. Count logical condition (flights delayed > 60 minutes)
not_cancelled %>%
  summarize(delayed_over_1hr = sum(arr_delay > 60, na.rm = TRUE))

```

```{r}
# 7. Proportion of flights delayed more than 60 minutes
not_cancelled %>%
  summarize(prop_delayed = mean(arr_delay > 60, na.rm = TRUE))

```

```{r}
delays <- not_cancelled %>%
group_by(tailnum) %>%
summarize(
delay = mean(arr_delay, na.rm = TRUE),
n = n()
)
ggplot(data = delays, mapping = aes(x = n, y = delay)) +
geom_point(alpha = 1/10)
```

```{r}
delays %>%
filter(n > 25) %>%
ggplot(mapping = aes(x = n, y = delay)) +
geom_point(alpha = 1/10)
```
